mission.title: Install and Configure Kubean on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubean is a production-ready cluster lifecycle management toolchain based on Kubespray, designed to simplify the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. Installing Kubean allows you to leverage its powerful lifecycle management features for your Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the Kubean Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kubean Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kubean https://kubean-io.github.io/kubean/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubean using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kubean with a specific version and create a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install kubean kubean/kubean --version 0.32.4 --namespace kubean --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Kubean pods are running successfully in the kubean namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubean
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Kubean operator
steps.description:
Update the Kubean operator deployment to set resource limits. You can edit the Helm release to include resource limits.
```bash
helm upgrade kubean kubean/kubean --namespace kubean --set kubeanOperator.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set kubeanOperator.resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Kubean
steps.description:
Ensure that the necessary RBAC permissions are set up for Kubean. You can create a service account and bind it to the cluster role.
```bash
kubectl create serviceaccount kubean-sa -n kubean
kubectl create clusterrolebinding kubean-sa --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=kubean:kubean-sa
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubean will show all pods running in the kubean namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods -n kubean`. Additionally, the resource limits and RBAC configurations will be applied as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubean https://kubean-io.github.io/kubean/charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubean kubean/kubean --namespace kubean --set kubeanOperator.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set kubeanOperator.resources.limits.memory=512Mi

kubectl create serviceaccount kubean-sa -n kubean
kubectl create clusterrolebinding kubean-sa --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=kubean:kubean-sa

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
